title: The World of Butane: An In-depth Look at C₄H₁₀
summary: Explore the chemical structure, isomerism, physical properties, and real-world applications of butane (C₄H₁₀) through an engaging high-quality educational video.

sections.heading: Molecular Structure and Formula
sections.body: A dynamic animation illustrates butane's zig-zag carbon chain with hydrogen atoms, displaying the molecule’s structural formula C₄H₁₀ clearly on screen.
key_points: Butane consists of four carbon atoms bonded in a straight line
Each end and interior carbon atom is tetrahedrally surrounded by hydrogen atoms

sections.heading: Isomerism: n-Butane vs. Isobutane (2-Methylpropane)
sections.body: A side-by-side 3D comparison clearly shows the structural difference between isobutane and n-butane, with highlighted branching in isobutane.
key_points: n-Butane has a straight chain structure
Isobutane (2-methylpropane) has one carbon atom bonded to three other carbons

sections.heading: Physical Properties and Combustion
sections.body: An infographic animation explains butane's properties, such as being a colorless gas that boils at –0.5°C; the video then demonstrates its safe combustion in controlled lab conditions.
key_points: Balanced chemical equation for complete butane combustion: 2C₄H₁₀ + 13O₂ → 8CO₂ + 10H₂O

sections.heading: Real-World Applications of Butane
sections.body: The video showcases butane's use in LPG cylinders, portable camping stoves, and as an aerosol propellant.
key_points: Butane serves as a fuel source for various applications
Applications are highlighted with clean transitions and modern motion graphics

key_facts: Despite differing structures, both isomers of butane have the same molecular formula C₄H₁₀.
visual_style: Bright and realistic colors with smooth transitions to maintain scientific accuracy
